steps.title: Identify the density-volume relationship
steps.content:
To find mass, we use:
$$\text{Mass} = \text{Density} \times \text{Volume}$$
Given:
  • Edge of cube = 3.000 cm
  • Density = 0.220 g/cm³
For a cube, Volume = (edge)³.

steps.title: Calculate volume and mass
steps.content:
First, compute the volume:
$$(3.000)^3 = 27.000 \text{ cm}^3$$
Then multiply by density:
$$\text{Mass} = 0.220 \times 27.000 = 5.94 \text{ grams}$$
